title: Big Price Rise and Space Adventure News
summary: This news article talks about two main stories. First, prices for things like gas are going up fast, which is called inflation. A report says prices rose 3.3% from last year, the biggest increase since 2024. This makes people worried about money. Second, astronauts are coming back to Earth after a trip to the moon. They are in a capsule that will splash down in the ocean near San Diego. NASA played songs to wake them up, and the astronauts are brave. The article also mentions a new arch plan in Washington, D.C., but focuses on prices and space. It helps us understand what's happening in the world today.

keywords.explanation: when prices for things go up over time
the cost of fuel for cars and other vehicles
when a spacecraft lands in the ocean
the group that sends people to space in the U.S.

questions.question: Where are the astronauts splashing down?
Who woke up the astronauts with songs?
What is the biggest price increase since?

background_read: Inflation is when things cost more money over time, like toys or food. Gas prices are what you pay for fuel to drive a car. Astronauts are explorers who go to space in rockets, and NASA is the American space agency that sends them. Splashdown is how some spacecraft return to Earth by landing in water. This article shares news about money and space adventures to help kids learn about current events.
Article_Structure: WHAT: Prices are rising fast and astronauts are returning from the moon
WHERE: In the U.S. and near San Diego
WHY: To inform about inflation and space mission updates

perspectives.description: People are worried because rising prices make it harder to buy things.
The astronauts' return is an exciting event showing human exploration.
